content.problemStatement:
A palindrome is a string that is identical to its reverse. For example, "aba", "abba", "a" and "aa" are palindromes, while "ab", "abb" and "xyz" are not.

A string XXX is called a substring of string YYY if it is possible to obtain XXX by erasing some (possibly zero) characters from the beginning of YYY and some (possibly zero) from the end of YYY without changing the order of the remaining characters. For example, "cp" is a substring of "icpc", while "icc" is not.

You are given two strings sss and ttt. Find the minimum length of a string (let's denote it by www) that satisfies the following conditions:


www is a palindrome
sss and ttt are substrings of www
constraints: sss and ttt contain only lowercase English letters
the sum of the lengths of all the strings on the input does not exceed 4,0004,0004,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated strings sss and ttt.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the minimum length of a palindrome that contains both sss and ttt as substrings.

sampleTestCases.input:
4
a a
a b
abc cb
abbc ba
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: sss and ttt are both "a", and "a" is also the shortest palindrome that contains both sss and ttt.
Example case 2: One possible string www is "aba", since there is no palindrome with length 111 or 222 that contains both "a" and "b".
Example case 3: One possible string www is "abcba".
Example case 4: One possible string www is "abbcbba".

hidden.input:
4
a a
a b
abc cb
abbc ba
